title: Gentle Simmered Japanese Sea Bass Nabemono
description: A cultured and cozy Japanese dish featuring sea bass fillets, render-fat, and high-protein ingredients, served in a nabemono style. This dish is perfect for a cold winter night.
instructions: Rinse the sea bass fillets under cold water and pat them dry with paper towels.
Heat the rendered pork fat in a large clay pot or Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the sea bass fillets to the pot and sear them for 2 minutes on each side.
Add the soy sauce, sake, and mirin to the pot and bring the mixture to a simmer.
Reduce the heat to low and add the green onions and shimeji mushrooms to the pot.
Cover the pot with a lid and simmer the mixture for 10 minutes.
Remove the pot from the heat and stir in the sesame oil.
Serve the sea bass and nabemono mixture hot, garnished with additional green onions if desired.
